L1.area: Input validation and security utilities for request handling

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied file path input is insufficiently sanitized, allowing attackers to bypass protection mechanisms by crafting paths that start with a forward slash. This enables arbitrary file reading on the system, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive files, information disclosure, and denial of service.

L3.area: Path sanitization routines within the security utility module
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where path sanitization functions fail to reject absolute paths beginning with a forward slash on Windows. Attackers can craft paths that bypass the existing traversal protections, enabling arbitrary file reading, information disclosure, and potentially denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The sanitize_path and sanitize_path_from_endpoint functions in the security module do not reject paths starting with a forward slash, allowing attackers to bypass path traversal protections and read arbitrary files on Windows.
